MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-09-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF THE MCEVORY GROUP LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT JOHN HARRIS IS GRANTED AND PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR AND AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $641,636 PLUS PREJUDGMENT INTEREST FROM JANUARY 5, 2015. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE PER CCP 437C(H) IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS AS TO THE ASSERTED FACTS REGARDING FRAUDULENT INDUCEMENT ARE SUSTAINED. SEE ORDER FOR FULL TEXT. ORDER SIGNED. SEPARATE ORDER RE: EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Apr-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Eisenbach of Cooley LLP telephone number 415-693-2094 for Plaintiff The McEvory Group LLC.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF THE MCEVORY GROUP LLC'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Defendant John Harris
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: ROBERT L. EISENBACH III, ESQ. of Cooley LLP, Tel No. 415-693-2094 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF THE MCEVORY GROUP LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Defendant John Harris is granted and plaintiff is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendant in the amount of $641,636 plus prejudgment interest from January 5, 2015. Plaintiff sustained its initial burden and defendant failed to present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue. Defendant's request for a continuance per CCP 437c(h) is denied. Plaintiff is not required to establish that it didn't breach the guaranty agreement. Moreover, the guaranty agreement permissibly limits defendant to defending against the guaranty only on the grounds of payment of the underlying debt. Defendant does not dispute that the lump sum amount of $641,636 is the correct amount due. Defendant has not presented any admissible evidence on his defense that he was fraudulently induced to enter into the guaranty agreement. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections as to the asserted facts regarding fraudulent inducement are sustained. Defendant has not shown that Mr. Oronzi is aware of facts that would create a triable issue on his fraudulent inducement defense. However, if defendant believes he can submit a declaration from Mr. Oronzi that does create such a triable issue, defendant can file a motion for reconsideration within the time frame permitted by that statute. ORDER SIGNED. SEPARATE ORDER RE: EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2015 Set For Trial On Mar-14-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
